input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:
`.testsettings` files are **not supported in MSTest v3**. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the mapping from your current settings:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property | `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| `AssemblyResolution` / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Code Coverage DataCollector | `<DataCollector>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

Let me create the replacement file:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory deployment -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
    <!-- Replaces <Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the Code Coverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment settings
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

With MSTest v3, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. The user needs to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file and explain the migration.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory deployment -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
    <!-- Replaces <Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" /> -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the Code Coverage DataCollector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire it up in the `.csproj` so it's picked up automatically:

Here's a summary of what was done:

**Created `local.runsettings`** with equivalent settings:
- **`<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>`** — replaces the `TestTimeout` property
- **`<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>`** — replaces the `AssemblyResolution`/`TestDirectory` deployment config (MSTest v3 runs in-place by default; set to `true` if you need file copying)
- **Code Coverage `<DataCollector>`** — uses the v2.0 URI format expected by modern tooling

**Updated `.csproj`** with `<RunSettingsFilePath>` so the settings are auto-discovered.

You can now safely delete `local.testsettings`. To run with coverage:

```
dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage"
```
